title: Feature Ticket Specification
description: Structured output from Business Analyst. Consumed by Technical Architect (for adr-spec.json), Technical Lead (for technical-delivery-plan.json), and Backend/Frontend Developers as the source of truth for scope, acceptance criteria, and business rules. Also optionally triggers Data Analyst and SEO Analyst handoffs.

ticket_id.description: Unique identifier. Convention: YYYY-MM-DD-short-slug or project-number.

business_value.description: Why this work matters to the business or users. Expressed as outcome, not activity.

preserved_behavior.description: Behavior that must remain unchanged after this change. Required for bug fixes.

changed_behavior.description: Intended new or restored behavior. Expressed in user/system terms.

actors.description: Human users, systems, or agent roles involved in this feature.

business_rules.description: Explicit business rules and invariants. Each rule must be testable.

rule.description: The business rule, stated as a declarative sentence.

exceptions.description: Known exceptions or edge cases to this rule.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable acceptance scenarios in Given/When/Then format.

scenario.description: Short label for this AC scenario.

edge_cases.description: Edge cases that require special handling but aren't full AC scenarios.

dependencies.description: Other tickets, services, or teams this work depends on.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded from this ticket to prevent scope creep.

open_questions.description: Questions requiring clarification before or during implementation.

process_impact.description: Downstream teams, systems, workflows, or users affected beyond the core change.

analytics_request.description: Optional handoff block for Data Analyst — triggers data investigation request.

decision_supported.description: What business decision will this data inform?

seo_content_request.description: Optional handoff block for SEO Analyst — triggers content or discoverability work.

must_link_to.description: High-value paths (product, property, guides) BA wants emphasized.

research_request.description: Optional handoff block for Researcher — triggers domain or compliance research.

examples.title: Change Order Assignment Logic for COD Store Orders
examples.business_value: Reduce failed COD delivery assignments by routing store orders to the nearest active store with COD capability, instead of the default warehouse courier route.
examples.preserved_behavior: Non-COD orders continue to use existing auto-assign logic unchanged.
examples.changed_behavior: COD orders with a store_id set are routed to the store's designated courier, bypassing warehouse assignment.

business_rules.rule: A COD order with store_id must be assigned to a courier linked to that store.
business_rules.exceptions: If no active courier is linked to the store, fall back to warehouse assignment and log a warning.

acceptance_criteria.scenario: COD order assigned to store courier
acceptance_criteria.given: A COD order exists with store_id=42 and store 42 has an active courier
acceptance_criteria.then: The order is assigned to the store 42 courier, not the warehouse courier

acceptance_criteria.scenario: COD order with no store courier falls back
acceptance_criteria.given: A COD order exists with store_id=99 and store 99 has no active courier
acceptance_criteria.then: The order is assigned to the warehouse courier and a warning is logged
The order follows the existing auto-assign logic without entering the new branch

edge_cases: store_id is set but store record is soft-deleted
COD order arrives during store courier capacity blackout window
dependencies: Store service API for courier lookup
open_questions: Should we notify the store manager when fallback occurs?
examples.process_impact: Store operations team will see different courier assignments for COD orders starting from deploy date.
